alignment.tuple_id: HLA-A|A*0201 POSITIVE STATUS|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy

paper_evidence.summary: The affinity-enhanced NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 TCR recognizes the SLLMWITQC peptide only in complex with HLA-A*0201. All treated patients were HLA-A*0201-positive; HLA-A*0201 positivity was an eligibility requirement for therapy.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: All 20 treated patients were HLA-A*0201-positive; responses (14/20 nCR/CR; median PFS 19.1 months, median OS 32.1 months) are reported only in this HLA-restricted cohort.
paper_evidence.evidence_type: PREDICTIVE (borderline between predictive vs pure eligibility criterion)
paper_evidence.evidence_level: B (single-arm phase I/II trial; could be argued as lower)

fields.evidence_description: HLA-A*0201 positive status is required for eligibility and response to NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy; all 20 treated patients were HLA-A201 positive with 70% CR/nCR and median PFS 19.1 months.
fields.verbatim_quote: We generated a human-derived affinity-enhanced TCR that recognizes the NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1-derived SLLMWITQC peptide in complex with HLA-A*0201.

field_status.evidence_type: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (the paper shows HLA-A*0201 is required for *treatment*, but not formally tested as a predictive biomarker vs HLA-A*0201-negative patients).
field_status.evidence_level: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (B may be slightly generous for a small single-arm trial).
field_status.evidence_direction: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (association of treatment in this HLA-positive group with responses).
field_status.evidence_significance: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (sensitivity is implied but no HLA-negative comparison).
field_status.evidence_description: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (correct narrative; slightly overstates causal predictive role).
field_status.verbatim_quote: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (the quoted sentence appears essentially verbatim in the text). :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}
field_status.identifiers: CORRECT_BUT_MISSING_IN_GROUND_TRUTH (Entrez, EFO, NCIt present only in AI).
field_status.variant_specifics: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (left null; no HGVS or coordinates exist in paper).

ai_items.notes: Scientifically valid as an HLA restriction/eligibility criterion. Labeling it as a predictive biomarker is somewhat interpretive but not strictly wrong in a clinical implementation sense.

alignment.tuple_id: NY-ESO-1 (CTAG1B)|OVEREXPRESSION/EXPRESSION|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy + ASCT + Melphalan

paper_evidence.summary: In HLA-A*0201+ MM patients with NY-ESO-1 and/or LAGE-1 expression, NY-ESO-259 TCR T-cell therapy after high-dose melphalan and ASCT led to high response rates and durable PFS/OS.
paper_evidence.location: Abstract; Results – Clinical Outcomes; Fig. 4; Fig. 6
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: 20 treated patients; 16/20 (80%) with advanced MM expressing NY-ESO-1 and/or LAGE-1; 14/20 (70%) achieved nCR/CR at day 100; median PFS 19.1 months (95% CI lower 8.5, upper not reached); median OS 32.1 months at later follow-up.

fields.evidence_description: In HLA-A*0201 positive MM patients with NY-ESO-1 expression, NY-ESO-259 TCR T-cells after high-dose melphalan and ASCT produced 70% nCR/CR and median PFS 19.1 months; median OS 32.1 months; clinical responses were durable and associated with persistent engineered T-cells.
fields.verbatim_quote: Of the 20 patients, 14/20 (70%) patients had a near complete response (nCR) or complete response (CR).

field_status.feature_names: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (NY-ESO-1 is a core target antigen).
field_status.variant_names: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (paper does not quantify 'overexpression' vs 'expression'; both are reasonable encodings).
field_status.therapy_names: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (paper treats NY-ESO-259 TCR product as main therapy; melphalan/ASCT is conditioning—AI listing them explicitly is acceptable but slightly different from CIViC style).
field_status.evidence_description: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (narrative and numbers match the Clinical Outcomes section).
field_status.identifiers: CORRECT_BUT_MISSING_IN_GROUND_TRUTH (Entrez, EFO, NCIt, RxCUI not present in GT).
field_status.variant_specifics: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (no sequence-level variants described in paper).

ai_items.notes: This AI item is essentially an expanded version of the CTAG1B CIViC item, adding conditioning regimen and multiple ontology IDs.

fields.evidence_description: Clinical responses to NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 TCR-engineered T cells with median PFS 19.1 months.
fields.source_title: NY-ESO-1-specific TCR-engineered T cells mediate sustained antigen-specific antitumor effects in myeloma.

gt_items.notes: GT and AI agree on core semantics (NY-ESO-1 overexpression → sensitivity to NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 TCR T cells), with AI providing extra regimen detail and identifiers.

alignment.tuple_id: LAGE-1 (CTAG2)|OVEREXPRESSION/EXPRESSION|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy + ASCT

paper_evidence.summary: LAGE-1 is a related cancer-testis antigen recognized by the same TCR; expression in myeloma cells (often more frequent than NY-ESO-1) identifies patients eligible for the same NY-ESO-259 TCR therapy, which shows high response rates.
paper_evidence.location: Introduction; Results – Patient screening & antigen expression
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: LAGE-1 expression frequency approximately twice that of NY-ESO-1; 34% of HLA-A2-positive screened patients expressed NY-ESO-1 and/or LAGE-1 and were eligible for treatment. Clinical responses and PFS/OS are reported at the cohort level.

fields.evidence_description: LAGE-1 expression in HLA-A*0201+ MM patients is associated with eligibility for NY-ESO-259 TCR T-cell therapy and clinical benefit (median PFS 19.1 months).
fields.verbatim_quote: LAGE-1 expression frequency was approximately twice that of NY-ESO-1.

field_status.variant_names: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (as with NY-ESO-1, 'expression' vs 'overexpression' is not formally distinguished).
field_status.therapy_names: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (again mixing product with ASCT but essentially accurate).
field_status.evidence_significance: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (paper reports benefit at cohort level for NY-ESO-1 and/or LAGE-1 expressers combined).
field_status.evidence_description: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (correct high-level story, but uses overall median PFS rather than a LAGE-1-only subgroup).
field_status.study_details: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (cohort_size is global TCR cohort, not LAGE-1-only subgroup).

ai_items.notes: Reasonable LAGE-1 predictive item; more granular subgroup outcomes are not reported, so some extrapolation is inevitable.

fields.evidence_description: Same trial-level response and PFS data applied to LAGE-1 overexpression.

gt_items.notes: GT and AI are aligned on semantics; AI adds identifiers and regimen context.

alignment.tuple_id: NY-ESO-1|LOSS/NEGATIVE SUBCLONES|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy

paper_evidence.summary: Loss of NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 antigen transcripts and emergence of antigen-negative subclones in marrow accompanies or precedes disease relapse in some patients, suggesting antigen escape under TCR therapy.
paper_evidence.location: Results – Evidence for NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 tumor antigen targeting; Fig. 5; Supplementary Table 8
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Loss of NY-ESO-1 and LAGE-1 transcripts observed in 12/15 patients at day 100 and 11/13 at day 180; 4 patients showed increased CD138 with absent NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 transcripts; 2 patients relapsed with NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 antigen-negative subclones; no antigen-positive relapse observed when engineered cells ≥6 cells/μl.
paper_evidence.evidence_level: B–C (small single-arm cohort with translational correlative data)

fields.evidence_description: Loss of NY-ESO-1 expression (with persistent or recurrent CD138+ myeloma) is associated with resistance and disease progression after NY-ESO-259 TCR T-cell therapy; two patients relapsed with NY-ESO-1 antigen-negative subclones and no antigen-positive relapse occurred when engineered cells persisted at ≥6 cells/μl.
fields.verbatim_quote: In no cases was an antigen-positive tumor relapse observed in the presence of engineered cells at ≥6 cells/μl; 2 patients relapsed with NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 antigen-negative subclones.

field_status.feature_names: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (the paper usually discusses NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 together; AI isolates NY-ESO-1).
field_status.evidence_level: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (B may be slightly high; data are from correlative analysis rather than a formal comparative study).
field_status.evidence_description: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (faithful to the text and tables).
field_status.identifiers: CORRECT_BUT_MISSING_IN_GROUND_TRUTH (GT has no explicit resistance items).
field_status.study_details: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (uses cohort_size=20 instead of the specific subsets 15/13/10 for biomarker analyses).

ai_items.notes: Good resistance/escape item; might ideally encode the combined NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 phenotype rather than NY-ESO-1 alone.

alignment.tuple_id: LAGE-1|LOSS/NEGATIVE SUBCLONES|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy

paper_evidence.summary: As above, loss of LAGE-1 (with NY-ESO-1) transcripts and emergence of antigen-negative subclones are part of an antigen-escape pattern under therapy.
paper_evidence.location: Results – Evidence for NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 tumor antigen targeting; Fig. 5; Supplementary Table 8
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Same as previous tuple; antigen-negative relapses are jointly NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 negative.

fields.evidence_description: Loss of LAGE-1 expression with persistent or recurrent myeloma (CD138+) is associated with resistance and antigen escape after NY-ESO-259 TCR T-cell therapy.
fields.verbatim_quote: loss of NY-ESO-1 and LAGE-1 transcripts was observed in 12/15 patients at day 100, and in 11/13 at day 180... 4 patients had an increase in CD138 transcripts in the absence of NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 transcripts.

ai_items.notes: Complementary to the NY-ESO-1 loss item; together they cover antigen-loss escape which GT lacks.

alignment.tuple_id: NY-ESO-1|EXPRESSION|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy + Lenalidomide

paper_evidence.summary: In patient 253, after second NY-ESO-259 TCR infusion (without second ASCT), low-dose lenalidomide started at day +24 was followed by marked CD8+ infiltration and reduced plasmacytosis with myeloma necrosis, suggesting possible immunomodulatory synergy and antigenic spreading.
paper_evidence.location: Results – T-cell persistence, trafficking, and function; Fig. 3; Supplementary Fig. 4 (text); case description for patient 253.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: Qualitative case: day +24 marrow with reduced CD138+ cells and increased CD8+ T cells; low-dose lenalidomide 10 mg/day started day +24; by day +38 further CD8+ increase and extensive CD138+ necrosis.

fields.evidence_description: In patient 253, second NY-ESO-259 TCR T-cell infusion followed by low-dose lenalidomide (10 mg/day) was associated with increased CD8+ T-cell infiltration and marked reduction of marrow plasmacytosis with CD138+ cell necrosis, suggesting lenalidomide may enhance T-cell–mediated anti-myeloma activity.
fields.verbatim_quote: Low dose lenalidomide (10 mg/day) was started on day +24. Two weeks later on day +38, the marrow biopsy demonstrated a considerable increase in the abundance of CD8+ T-cells concomitant with a marked decrease in marrow plasmacytosis with extensive CD138+ myeloma cell necrosis.

field_status.therapy_names: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (NY-ESO-259 and lenalidomide both explicitly described).

ai_items.notes: Nice example of AI surfacing combination-therapy synergy; absent from GT and clearly grounded in the text.

alignment.tuple_id: NY-ESO-1|LONGITUDINAL EXPRESSION LEVEL|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy

paper_evidence.summary: Higher longitudinal NY-ESO-1 expression in marrow is associated with worse overall survival and possibly shorter PFS; NY-ESO-1 expression is also correlated with high-risk disease features.
paper_evidence.location: Supplementary Tables 9–11; text summarizing joint models; Introduction (high-risk correlation).
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: T-cell persistence in blood inversely correlated with NY-ESO-1 expression (p=0.022); longitudinal NY-ESO-1 expression associated with OS (p=0.037) and may be predictive of PFS (p=0.094).

fields.evidence_description: Higher levels of NY-ESO-1 expression over time in marrow were significantly associated with worse overall survival and potentially shorter progression-free survival in the joint models; NY-ESO-1 expression is correlated with tumor proliferation and high-risk features.
fields.verbatim_quote: longitudinal expression of NY-ESO-1 and LAGE-1 was associated with OS, and the corresponding p-values for their effects in the model are 0.037 and 0.055, respectively.

field_status.therapy_names: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (prognostic effect is observed in the treated cohort; leaving therapy blank is not wrong).
field_status.evidence_level: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (B is too strong; C would better reflect exploratory modeling).
field_status.evidence_description: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (correctly reflects association with OS and trend with PFS).
field_status.study_details: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (small N; AI doesn’t emphasize sample size limitations).

ai_items.notes: Reasonable prognostic item; the only real issue is slightly inflated evidence level.

fields.evidence_description: In the treated cohort, higher NY-ESO-1 expression is inversely correlated with T-cell persistence and associated with poorer outcomes (shorter OS) in joint models.
fields.verbatim_quote: Between days 0 through 180 post-transplant the T-cell persistence in blood was inversely correlated with the level of NY-ESO-1 expression in the marrow (p=0.022).

field_status.therapy_names: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (this item is correctly contextualized as therapy-specific).
field_status.evidence_description: PARTIAL_MATCH_OR_UNDER-SPECIFIED (inference that reduced persistence causally mediates poorer outcomes is plausible but not formally proven).

ai_items.notes: Provides a more mechanistic view (antigen level ↔ T-cell persistence ↔ outcome). Scientifically sensible, though causal claims should be phrased cautiously.

alignment.tuple_id: LAGE-1|LONGITUDINAL EXPRESSION LEVEL|Multiple Myeloma|NY-ESO-259 TCR-engineered T-cell therapy

paper_evidence.summary: Higher longitudinal LAGE-1 expression is marginally associated with worse OS and may be predictive of shorter PFS.
paper_evidence.location: Supplementary Tables 9–11; text summarizing joint models.
paper_evidence.quantitative_data: OS association p=0.055; possible PFS prediction p=0.065.

fields.evidence_description: LAGE-1 expression levels over time showed marginal association with worse overall survival and trends toward shorter progression-free survival.
fields.verbatim_quote: longitudinal expression of NY-ESO-1 and LAGE-1 was associated with OS, and the corresponding p-values for their effects in the model are 0.037 and 0.055, respectively.

field_status.evidence_description: EXACT_MATCH_WITH_PAPER (captures the marginal nature of associations).

ai_items.notes: Consistent with the exploratory joint modeling; overstates certainty by choosing level B.

missing_from_ai: No clearly distinct, clinically actionable gene–variant–therapy tuples appear to be completely missing. One could optionally curate a combined 'NY-ESO-1 and/or LAGE-1' expression item instead of (or in addition to) separate CTAG1B and CTAG2 entries, but the AI’s split representation is still consistent with the paper.
ai_hallucinated_items: None of the 9 AI evidence items are truly unsupported by the paper. The main issues are degree (inflated evidence level B vs C/D) and semantics (calling eligibility criteria 'predictive' and inferring slightly stronger causal statements than the data strictly prove).
ai_improvements_over_gt: AI adds explicit items for antigen-loss–mediated resistance (NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 transcript loss and antigen-negative relapses), which are well-supported in the paper but absent from CIViC curation.
AI captures exploratory prognostic modeling (longitudinal NY-ESO-1 and LAGE-1 expression associated with OS and possibly PFS), again supported by supplementary tables but not represented in GT.
AI surfaces a case-level lenalidomide combination signal (patient 253) with coherent fields (cohort_size=1, evidence_level D), which GT ignores.

AI introduces richer ontology mapping (Entrez IDs, EFO disease IDs, NCIt therapy IDs, RxCUI codes) and structured study details (cohort_size, source_page_numbers) that are not present in GT but align with the trial as described.

overall_judgement.summary: The AI extraction for PMID 26193344 is high-quality and largely faithful to the paper: it recovers the core CIViC predictive items, adds well-supported resistance and prognostic biomarkers, and encodes identifiers and study details in a useful way. Its main weaknesses are slightly inflated evidence levels for exploratory analyses and treating some inclusion/eligibility constraints as fully validated predictive biomarkers.
strengths: Accurately reproduces key response outcomes (14/20 nCR/CR; median PFS 19.1 months; median OS 32.1 months) and aligns with GT predictive items for CTAG1B and CTAG2.
Surfaces clinically meaningful resistance mechanisms (antigen loss and antigen-negative relapse) with correct direction and good textual grounding.
Captures exploratory OS/PFS biomarker modeling with correct p-values and directions, and provides rich ontology and study metadata beyond GT.
weaknesses: Evidence levels sometimes too optimistic (B instead of C/D for small, exploratory biomarker models).
Some semantic overreach: treating HLA-A*0201 status and eligibility constraints as fully established predictive biomarkers.
Subtle nuances (combined NY-ESO-1/LAGE-1 expression vs gene-specific effects, small-sample limits) are not always clearly communicated in the evidence_description fields.
